objective: Architectural sign-off on the full-screen ratatui config TUI + `grove config` verb, confirming layer boundaries, cross-cutting impact, and deployment posture.
key_changes: Layer boundary held: ratatui/crossterm confined to cli/Cargo.toml; core stays TUI-free (cli formats, core executes).
Validation/persistence delegated to core ExploreConfig::validate()/save(); atomic temp+rename write preserved.
Elm-style pure update() covered by 12 headless tests — durable regression surface with no terminal needed.
Single source of truth: provider URL constants (Ollama 11434, llama.cpp 8080) match core; dirty_url guards user-edited endpoints.
Safe failure modes: non-TTY fast-fail (exit 1) and terminal restore on all exit paths incl. save error.
AC #5 (model auto-discovery) correctly scoped out via #[allow(dead_code)] Msg::ModelListFetched extension point.
No cross-cutting impact: additive verb + new module; no existing CLI/MCP/core surface modified; no config schema change.
Operational impact minimal: no version bump, no migration, backward compatible; binary size 13,296,264 bytes recorded per AC #6.
Follow-up: add promised non-TTY assert_cmd integration test in cli/tests/cli.rs (verified manually, not automated).
Follow-up: wire Msg::ModelListFetched to provider model-list endpoints for AC #5 in a future sprint.
Follow-up: consider Provider::default_base_url() helper in core to unify TUI URL constants with core defaults.
